Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire-to-Retire Process: 1. Post Job Ad 2. Screen Candidates 3. Conduct Interviews 4. Make Hiring Decision 5. Onboard Employee 6. Add to Payroll 7. Close Case The standard sequence should be sequential, possibly with some parallelism or loops where logically appropriate.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets look at Model 1: Nodes: Post, Screen, Decide, Interview, Onboard, Payroll, Close Edges: Post -> Screen Screen -> Decide Screen -> Interview Decide -> Onboard Onboard -> Payroll Payroll -> Close trace the possible executions in Model 1. Its a StrictPartialOrder, so any topological sort of the graph is a valid execution. Decide can happen BEFORE Interview. This is a severe anomaly: making a hiring decision before conducting interviews!

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets look at Model 2: Nodes: Post, Screen, Interview, Decide, loop_onboarding, xor_payroll, Close loop_onboarding = LOOP(Onboard, skip) xor_payroll = XOR(Payroll, skip) Edges: Post -> Screen Post -> Interview Interview -> Decide Decide -> loop_onboarding loop_onboarding -> xor_payroll xor_payroll -> Close Post -> Screen and Post -> Interview. There is no edge between Screen and Interview! This means Screening and Interviews can happen in parallel, or Interviews can happen BEFORE Screening. This is an anomaly.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Evaluate the severity of anomalies in Model 1: 1. Decide can happen before or concurrent with Interview. Very severe. In Model 2: 1. Interview can happen before or concurrent with Screen. Bad practice, less severe. 2. XOR(Payroll, skip) means adding to payroll is optional. Severe business anomaly. 3. LOOP(Onboard, skip) means onboarding can be repeated. Moderate anomaly.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Which model more closely aligns with a normative version? Model 1 gets the mandatory nature right but destroys Interview -> Decide. Model 2 gets Interview -> Decide right but has optional payroll and loop. Wait, lets reconsider. Arguing for Model 1: mandatory payroll crucial. Model 2 explicitly models incorrect skip. Wait, double check Model 1 edges. Yes, no edge between Interview and Decide.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 Anomalies: 1. Missing edge between Interview and Decide: allows hiring decision before interviews. Severe. Model 2 Anomalies: 1. Missing edge between Screen and Interview: allows interviews before screening. Moderate. 2. Optional Payroll XOR(Payroll, skip): severe. 3. Looping Onboarding: moderate.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Conclusion: Model 1 more closely aligns with the normative version. While its missing edge allows an illogical execution, it does not explicitly build in a path that bypasses mandatory business activities like payroll. Model 2s XOR(Payroll, skip) is a direct and severe violation of business integrity, and the LOOP(Onboard, skip) is structurally far from the normative one-time onboarding step.
